content:
# Matthew Chapter 26 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_026_page_001.jpg, MAT_026_page_002.jpg, MAT_026_page_003.jpg

1. And it came to pass [that] when the Yeshua finished all these words, he said to the disciples of him,

2. "You know that after two days the Pascha comes, and the Son of the Man is delivered up to be crucified."

3. Then the chief priests and the elders of the people were gathered into the courtyard of the chief priest, the [one] called Kaiaphas,

4. and they took counsel together that they might seize the Yeshua by deceit and kill [him].

5. But they said, "Not during the feast, lest there be a disturbance among the people."

6. And the Yeshua having come to be in Bethania, in [the] house of Simon the leper,

7. a woman came to him having an alabaster [jar] of very costly perfumed-oil, and she poured [it] on the head of him [as he was] reclining.

8. And seeing [this], the disciples were indignant, saying, "For what [purpose is] this waste?

9. For this could have been sold for much and given to [the] poor."

10. But the Yeshua, knowing, said to them, "Why do you cause trouble for the woman? For she worked a good work toward me.

11. For the poor you have with you always, but me you do not have always.

12. For she, having cast this perfumed-oil upon the body of me, did [it] for the burial of me.

13. Truly I say to you, wherever this gospel is proclaimed in the whole world, also what this [woman] did will be spoken of for a memorial of her."

14. Then one of the twelve, the [one] called Ioudas Iskariotes, having gone to the chief priests,

15. said, "What are you willing to give me, and I will deliver him to you?" And they set for him thirty silver [pieces].

16. And from then he was seeking an opportunity that he might deliver him.

17. And on the first [day] of the Unleavened [Bread], the disciples came to the Yeshua, saying, "Where do you wish [that] we prepare for you to eat the Pascha?"

18. And he said, "Go into the city to a certain [man] and say to him, 'The Teacher says, the time of me is near; with you I do the Pascha with the disciples of me.'"

19. And the disciples did as the Yeshua directed them, and they prepared the Pascha.

20. And evening having come, he was reclining with the twelve.

21. And [while] they were eating, he said, "Truly I say to you that one of you will deliver me up."

22. And being exceedingly grieved, each one of them began to say to him, "Surely not I, Lord?"

23. And answering, he said, "The [one] having dipped the hand with me in the dish, this [one] will deliver me up.

24. The Son of the Man goes just as it has been written concerning him, but woe to the man that through whom the Son of the Man is delivered up. It were good for him if the man that had not been born."

25. And answering, Ioudas, the [one] delivering him up, said, "Surely not I, Rabbi?" He says to him, "You said [it]."

26. And [while] they were eating, the Yeshua, having taken bread and having blessed, broke [it] and giving to the disciples said, "Take, eat; this is the body of me."

27. And having taken a cup and having given thanks, he gave [it] to them, saying, "Drink from it, all [of you].

28. For this is the blood of me of the covenant, the [blood] being poured out for many for forgiveness of sins.

29. And I say to you, I will not drink from now from this fruit of the vine until the day that when I drink it new with you in the kingdom of the Father of me."

30. And having sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of the Olives.

31. Then the Yeshua says to them, "All of you will be scandalized in me in this night, for it has been written, 'I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.'

32. But after I am raised, I will go before you into the Galilaia."

33. And answering, the Petros said to him, "If all will be scandalized in you, I will never be scandalized."

34. The Yeshua said to him, "Truly I say to you that in this night, before [the] rooster crows, three times you will deny me."

35. The Petros says to him, "Even if it is necessary for me to die with you, I will not deny you." Likewise also said all the disciples.

36. Then the Yeshua comes with them to a place called Gethsemane, and he says to the disciples, "Sit here while going away there I pray."

37. And having taken along the Petros and the two sons of Zebedaios, he began to be grieved and distressed.

38. Then he says to them, "The soul of me is deeply grieved to [the point of] death. Remain here and watch with me."

39. And having gone forward a little, he fell upon the face of him, praying and saying, "Father of me, if it is possible, let this cup pass from me; but not as I will, but as you [will]."

40. And he comes to the disciples and finds them sleeping, and he says to the Petros, "So you were not able [for] one hour to watch with me?

41. Watch and pray, that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit [is] willing, but the flesh [is] weak."

42. Again a second [time], going away, he prayed, saying, "Father of me, if this cannot pass unless I drink it, let the will of you be done."

43. And coming again, he found them sleeping, for the eyes of them were heavy.

44. And leaving them, going away again, he prayed a third [time], saying the same word again.

45. Then he comes to the disciples and says to them, "Sleep the remaining [time] and rest. Behold, the hour has drawn near, and the Son of the Man is delivered into [the] hands of sinners.

46. Rise, let us go. Behold, the [one] delivering me up has drawn near."

47. And while he was still speaking, behold, Ioudas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great crowd with swords and clubs from the chief priests and elders of the people.

48. And the [one] delivering him up gave them a sign, saying, "Whomever I kiss, he is [the one]; seize him."

49. And immediately coming to the Yeshua, he said, "Greetings, Rabbi!" and kissed him.

50. And the Yeshua said to him, "Friend, [do] that for which you are here." Then coming, they laid the hands upon the Yeshua and seized him.

51. And behold, one of the [ones] with the Yeshua, stretching out the hand, drew the sword of him and striking the slave of the chief priest, cut off the ear of him.

52. Then the Yeshua says to him, "Return the sword of you to the place of it, for all the [ones] having taken [the] sword will perish by [the] sword.

53. Or do you think that I am not able to call upon the Father of me, and he will provide for me now more than twelve legions of angels?

54. How then would the scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must happen?"

55. In that hour the Yeshua said to the crowds, "As against a robber you came out with swords and clubs to seize me? Daily I was sitting in the temple teaching, and you did not seize me.

56. But this whole [thing] has happened that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled." Then all the disciples, leaving him, fled.

57. And the [ones] having seized the Yeshua led [him] away to Kaiaphas the chief priest, where the scribes and the elders were gathered.

58. And the Petros was following him from afar, up to the courtyard of the chief priest, and entering inside, he sat with the attendants to see the end.

59. And the chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were seeking false testimony against the Yeshua so that they might put him to death,

60. and they did not find [any], many false witnesses having come forward. But at last two came forward

61. and said, "This [one] said, 'I am able to destroy the temple of the God and after three days to build it.'"

62. And standing up, the chief priest said to him, "Do you answer nothing? What [is it that] these testify against you?"

63. But the Yeshua was silent. And the chief priest said to him, "I adjure you by the living God that you tell us if you are the Christos, the Son of the God."

64. The Yeshua says to him, "You said [it]. But I say to you, from now you will see the Son of the Man sitting at [the] right of the Power and coming upon the clouds of the heaven."

65. Then the chief priest tore the garments of him, saying, "He blasphemed! What further need have we of witnesses? Behold, now you heard the blasphemy.

66. What does it seem to you?" And answering, they said, "He is deserving of death."

67. Then they spat in the face of him and struck him with fists, and [some] slapped [him],

68. saying, "Prophesy to us, Christos! Who is the [one] having struck you?"

69. And the Petros was sitting outside in the courtyard, and one servant-girl came to him, saying, "You also were with the Yeshua the Galilaian."

70. But he denied before all, saying, "I do not know what you say."

71. And [when] he went out to the gateway, another [girl] saw him and says to the [ones] there, "This [one] was with the Yeshua the Nazoraian."

72. And again he denied with an oath, "I do not know the man."

73. And after a little, the [ones] standing [by] came and said to the Petros, "Truly you also are of them, for the speech of you makes you evident."

74. Then he began to curse and to swear, "I do not know the man." And immediately a rooster crowed.

75. And the Petros remembered the word of the Yeshua having said, "Before [the] rooster crows, three times you will deny me." And going outside, he wept bitterly.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Πάσχα (vv.2, 17, 18, 19):** Transliterated from Hebrew/Aramaic פֶּסַח (Pesach), meaning "Passover." EMET renders it "Pascha" to preserve the Greek form.

2. **v.15 — "thirty silver [pieces]":** τριάκοντα ἀργύρια — fulfilling Zechariah 11:12. The price of a slave (Exodus 21:32). The betrayal price underscores the insult: Jesus is valued at the cost of a common slave.

3. **v.28 — "blood of the covenant":** Sinaiticus reads τὸ αἷμα μου τῆς διαθήκης ("the blood of me of the covenant"). Some later manuscripts add "new" (καινῆς) before "covenant." The Sinaiticus reading echoes Exodus 24:8 ("the blood of the covenant") directly.

4. **v.50 — "Friend, [do] that for which you are here":** ἑταῖρε, ἐφ' ὃ πάρει — a notoriously difficult phrase. It may be a question ("Friend, why are you here?") or a command ("Friend, do what you came to do"). The Greek is ambiguous. EMET renders it as a command, following the syntax.

5. **v.64 — "You said [it]":** σὺ εἶπας — Jesus' response to Caiaphas is identical in form to his response to Judas in v.25. It is an affirmation that places the confession on the questioner's lips rather than his own.

6. **v.71 — "the Nazoraian":** Sinaiticus reads Ναζωραίου, not Ναζαρηνού. "Nazoraian" may refer to Nazareth but could also carry theological overtones (cf. "Nazarite" or the Hebrew נֵצֶר, "branch" — Isaiah 11:1).

— EMET, AI Translator
